title: Ribollita Tuscan Bread Bean Soup

seo.title: How to Make Ribollita Tuscan Bread Bean Soup — Easy Italian Recipe
seo.metaDescription: Learn how to make authentic Ribollita Tuscan Bread Bean Soup. This easy Italian recipe includes step-by-step instructions, tips, and serving ideas.

easy Ribollita Tuscan Bread Bean Soup recipe
authentic Italian Ribollita Tuscan Bread Bean Soup

seo.imageAlt: Authentic Italian Ribollita Tuscan Bread Bean Soup recipe
seo.imageCaption: Delicious homemade Ribollita Tuscan Bread Bean Soup — a beloved Italian classic

description: Ribollita is a hearty Tuscan bread and vegetable soup, a true peasant dish that embodies the spirit of cucina povera. It is traditionally made by reheating (ribollire) leftover minestrone or vegetable soup with stale bread, creating a thick, comforting meal. This robust and flavorful soup is perfect for a satisfying lunch, showcasing the rich, earthy flavors of Tuscany.

instructions.title: Sauté Aromatics
instructions.text: In a large heavy-bottomed pot or Dutch oven, heat the olive oil over medium heat. Add the chopped onion, carrots, and celery. Sauté for 8-10 minutes, until the vegetables are softened and translucent. Add the minced garlic and cook for another 1-2 minutes until fragrant.

instructions.title: Add Tomatoes and Beans
instructions.text: Stir in the canned diced tomatoes with their juices and the rinsed and drained cannellini beans. Cook for 5 minutes, stirring occasionally, to allow the flavors to meld. Season with salt and black pepper.

instructions.title: Pour in Broth and Simmer
instructions.text: Pour in the vegetable broth. Bring the mixture to a gentle boil, then reduce the heat to low, cover, and let it simmer for 15 minutes. This allows the vegetables to become tender and the flavors to deepen.

instructions.title: Incorporate Kale
instructions.text: Add the chopped black kale (cavolo nero) to the pot. Stir well, ensuring the kale is submerged in the liquid. Continue to simmer, uncovered, for another 10-15 minutes, or until the kale is tender but still vibrant green.

instructions.title: Add Stale Bread
instructions.text: Stir in the torn pieces of stale rustic bread. Gently push the bread into the soup to soak up the liquid. Continue to cook for 5-7 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the bread has softened and thickened the soup. The soup should have a rustic, thick consistency.

instructions.title: Serve and Garnish
instructions.text: Taste and adjust seasoning as needed. Ladle the hot Ribollita into bowls. Drizzle each serving generously with extra virgin olive oil and sprinkle with freshly grated Parmesan cheese. Serve immediately, or even better, let it rest for a few hours or overnight for the flavors to fully develop before reheating.

tips: For the best flavor, allow the Ribollita to rest overnight and reheat it the next day. The flavors deepen beautifully, which is why it is called 'reboiled'.
If you cant find black kale (cavolo nero), Swiss chard or regular kale are good substitutes. Ensure you remove the tough stems before chopping.
Serve Ribollita with a drizzle of high-quality extra virgin olive oil and a sprinkle of freshly grated Parmesan cheese for an authentic Tuscan experience.
article: Ribollita Tuscan Bread Bean Soup is a beloved Italian Lunch Recipes dish enjoyed across Italian. This authentic recipe brings traditional flavors to your kitchen.
